AB.taxAuthority: Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) & Alberta Treasury Board and Finance

immediateExpensingClass50.detail: Under CRA Part XVII Immediate Expensing Provision, eligible Canadian-controlled private corporations (CCPCs) can deduct up to 100% of the cost of Class 50 computer equipment acquired after April 18, 2021, and before 2024, or claim 55% normal declining balance with 1.5x rule in the first year for other years.

allowableDeductions.description: Smart Espresso machines, IoT grinders, and automated POS systems integrated under geofencing fall under CCA Class 50. Immediate 100% tax write-off is eligible up to $1.5M yearly limit among associated CCPC groups.
allowableDeductions.complianceSection: CRA Income Tax Folio S3-F4-C1, General Business Expenses

allowableDeductions.description: Website development (kalimcoffee.ca), digital ordering kiosk subscriptions, and real-time cloud dashboard hosting fees are 100% deductible in the year they are incurred as current operating expenses.
allowableDeductions.complianceSection: CRA Income Tax Folio S3-F4-C1, Current vs. Capital Expenses

allowableDeductions.description: Commercial barista certifications, food safety training programs, and wages (including CPP/EI employer shares) are 100% deductible operating expenses.
allowableDeductions.complianceSection: CRA Guide T4001, Employer's Guide - Payroll Deductions and Remittances

allowableDeductions.description: Leasing kitchen equipment or range roast machinery allows 100% write-off of the monthly rental installments, moving capital expenses (CapEx) to operating expenses (OpEx) for smooth cash flow.

officialGuides: CRA Guide T4012 - T2 Corporation - Income Tax Guide
Alberta AT1 - Alberta Corporate Income Tax Return Guide
CRA Guide T4001 - Employer's Guide – Payroll Deductions
taxPlanningAdvice: Pay sweet-and-savory dividends to shareholders instead of salary to reduce payroll taxes (CPP/EI) for executive baristas, subject to corporate-level Tax Integration rules.
Ensure GST quarterly filings are completed by the 30th of the month following the reporting period to avoid 1% + 25% late penalties.
Take advantage of the zero provincial sales tax (PST) in Alberta to purchase high-end espresso equipment at lower up-front capital outlay.

BC.taxAuthority: Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) & BC Ministry of Finance

immediateExpensingClass50.detail: Under CRA and BC alignment, immediate expensing up to $1.5M for hardware and software assets is eligible for CCPCs. Helps offset the heavy capital investment in smart POS screens, GPS check-in devices, and integrated camera nodes.

allowableDeductions.description: In BC, if the company's total B.C. payroll is $500,000 or less, they are 100% exempt from the Employer Health Tax. For payroll between $500k and $1.5M, a notch rate applies. Plan staff roster and shifts to stay within the $500k exemption cap if operating a single café branch.
allowableDeductions.complianceSection: BC Employer Health Tax Act, SBC 2018

allowableDeductions.description: Most commercial food/beverage ingredients (beans, milk, sweeteners) are exempt from BC PST (7%). However, non-packaging paper cups, straws, cutlery, and operational technology equipment are subject to 7% PST but can be written off under capital cost allowances or general business expenses.

Investment in BC localized e-commerce tools, online food delivery portal configurations, and secure payment processing gateways on the server can be expensed directly or offset with provincial tech credits.
allowableDeductions.complianceSection: BC Small Business Venture Capital Act

officialGuides: CRA Guide T4012 - T2 Corporation - Income Tax Guide
BC Employer Health Tax Notice of Assessment and Payment Guide
taxPlanningAdvice: Keep a thorough record of GPS geofenced logs as audit proofs for employee work mileage and automated clock-ins, ensuring fully compliant tax deductions for staff-travel reimbursements.
Monitor BC EHT threshold across multiple branches. If combined group payroll exceeds $500k, associated corporations share the exemption amount.

ON.taxAuthority: Canada Revenue Agency (CRA) & Ontario Ministry of Finance

immediateExpensingClass50.detail: Ontario aligns with the federal immediate expensing limits, allowing local cafe locations to completely write off their digital inventory automation software, server integrations, and intelligent hardware instantly.

allowableDeductions.description: Ontario offers a very generous $1,000,000 exemption from Employer Health Tax for eligible private sector employers, meaning small cafe chains with less than $1M payroll pay $0 Ontario EHT.
allowableDeductions.complianceSection: Ontario Employer Health Tax Act, R.S.O. 1990

allowableDeductions.description: Reduces the provincial corporate tax rate on the first $500k of active business income down from 11.5% general rate to 3.2% small business rate.
Cost of delivering coffee goods, leasing and registering delivery vans, and reimbursing barista travel times via smart GPS logs can be fully expensed with 100% operating write-off.
allowableDeductions.complianceSection: CRA Guide T4002 - Business and Professional Income

officialGuides: CRA Guide T4012 - T2 Corporation - Income Tax Guide
Ontario Corporate Income Tax return (Schedule 500 equivalent)
Ontario Employer Health Tax Guide for Small Businesses
taxPlanningAdvice: Leverage the Input Tax Credit (ITC) mechanism to recover 13% HST paid on coffee shop lease, power supply, roasting machinery, and premium furniture.
Ensure all employee perks (like free gourmet croissants or daily lattes) conform to CRA administrative policy on non-taxable employee meals to prevent unexpected payroll tax audits.

US-CA.taxAuthority: Internal Revenue Service (IRS) & California Franchise Tax Board (FTB)

immediateExpensingClass50.detail: Under US Section 179, businesses can immediately expense up to $1.22M of eligible equipment (computers, POS systems, espresso gear). California conforms partially to Section 179 but maintains different depreciation pathways for regular items.

allowableDeductions.description: Direct deductibility of software, advanced point-of-sale platforms, and coffee roasting hardware instead of depreciating them over 5-7 years.
Store rent, water filters, packaging, food handler permit costs, employee state disability insurance (CASDI) payments, and marketing ads are 100% deductible.
Reduces federal tax liabilities when hiring baristas and workers from target veteran or barrier-to-work groups.

officialGuides: California FTB Publication 1060 - Guide for Corporations
taxPlanningAdvice: Plan your capital purchases carefully before Dec 31 to claim Section 179 benefits in the current tax year.

Maintain strict digital ledgers showing real-time tips tracking, as the IRS requires precise reporting of tip pools via Form 8027 (Employer's Annual Information Return of Tip Income) if total staff count exceeds threshold.
